summary: Established a minimal secure Electron Windows shell with explicit BrowserWindow security preferences, a narrow preload bridge, package/platform adapter types, root desktop security/package scripts, and automated assertions for renderer Node isolation and trusted renderer-root-only file navigation. The client_engineer writer subthread produced the initial shell but ended with an Unsupported content type tool error; lead_orchestrator integrated the partial work, tightened the trusted file URL boundary, and verified the required commands.

tests_run.result: Scope all 12 workspace projects; lockfile already up to date.

Built @monsoon/platform and apps/desktop; asserted nodeIntegration false, contextIsolation true, sandbox true, webSecurity true, insecure content disabled, narrow monsoonDesktop preload bridge, renderer has no require/ipcRenderer, and file navigation is restricted to the packaged renderer root.
Built desktop shell and generated a smoke package under .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64 with main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts.
Initial lead verification failed because apps/desktop/scripts/security-check.mjs needed Prettier formatting after the trusted file URL assertions were added.
Prettier, ESLint, recursive workspace typecheck, and architecture boundaries passed with desktop/platform sources included and generated dist excluded from root globs.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic Node and worker-compatible hash remained 61e0ba1c; root Vitest passed 1 file/2 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.

decisions: Implemented a self-contained desktop smoke package script instead of introducing Electron Forge in F006, keeping the shell minimal while still building main, preload, renderer, and platform artifacts.
Kept the preload API narrow: only getRuntimeInfo and setFullscreen are exposed through the named monsoonDesktop bridge; raw ipcRenderer is not exposed.
Added @monsoon/platform as a platform boundary package for DesktopPlatformBridge, PlatformAdapter types, IPC channel constants, locale/save-slot/external-url constructors, and request validation.
Kept apps/desktop thin and authority-free: Electron main/preload do not read or mutate game state.
Added Electron 42.4.1 only to apps/desktop devDependencies, matching the frozen technology stack support line.
Added root package scripts for pnpm desktop:security-check and pnpm desktop:package because they are the task's required tests.
Added dist/ to .gitignore and narrowed root lint/format globs so generated desktop/platform build outputs do not enter commits or quality checks.
risks: The desktop:package command is a smoke package, not a signed or distributable Electron installer. Release artifacts remain FOUNDATION-007 scope.
The client_engineer writer subthread errored with Unsupported content type after partial implementation; lead integrated and verified the resulting code before review.
The preload bridge currently exposes only runtime info and fullscreen control. Future file/save APIs must add runtime schema validation and security tests before exposure.
Renderer DOM smoke is minimal and not yet a Playwright-launched Electron runtime test; current F006 coverage is build-time and script-level security assertion.
requested_action: Perform read-only security review of the Electron security configuration, preload/API boundary, IPC channel set, package smoke, scope reconciliations, and command evidence. Return ACCEPT / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK.
